segments.title: Soya's icebreaker hull and later modifications
segments.summary: A first look at Soya draws attention to her icebreaker role and the conspicuous hull bulges that record later changes to the ship.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke identifies Soya as an icebreaker before the party begins its inspection. He immediately points out prominent bulges on the hull and describes them as permanent, historically interesting modifications. The moment is a useful reminder that preserved ships often show several phases of their working lives rather than a single original design. For viewers interested in ship design, it offers a clear visual prompt to ask how hull form, structural changes, and an icebreaking role relate to a vessel's later appearance. The video does not explain the technical purpose of the bulges, so this watch point preserves that limit rather than supplying an unsupported explanation.

evidence.note: The transcript identifies Soya as an icebreaker and calls out her permanent hull bulges as interesting modifications.
